blocks.content: The group by transformation allows you to group rows that share the same values in one or more fields. It's similar to the SQL `GROUP BY` clause. Use this when you need to aggregate data based on one or more fields, such as grouping by a specific category or region.
To use this transformation, open an existing panel in edit mode on your dashboard.

Select **Group by** on the dropdown of the field(s) you want to group by. To calculate a value for the other fields and apply it to each group, select **Calculate** and a calculation type on the dropdown of the field(s) you want to calculate.
Click **Save dashboard**, confirm the title, and click **Save**.

The transformation is applied automatically. If it doesn't refresh, click **Refresh** on the panel to verify the visualization looks as intended.

In the next milestone, you'll filter your data by specific values.

---

### More to explore (optional)

At this point in your journey, you can explore the following paths:

- [Group by](/docs/grafana/latest/panels-visualizations/query-transform-data/transform-data/#group-by)
